text.title: ES Aggregate Failure Modes
text.summary: Four antipatterns observed in ES aggregate implementations. Symptom + root cause + fix per item.

antipattern.symptom: Command body: `self.status = "placed"; self._pending.append(OrderPlaced(...))`. Replay through `apply` doesn't touch `status`; reconstructed state diverges from live state.
antipattern.root-cause: Author treated command as setter + side-effect.
antipattern.fix: Remove the direct assignment. The `_apply_OrderPlaced` handler is solely responsible for setting `self.status`. Command only emits the event.

antipattern.symptom: `repo.save(stream_id, events)` is called without `expected_version`. Two concurrent commands both append events; second one clobbers ordering; replay produces invalid state.
antipattern.root-cause: Repository didn't enforce or call site didn't pass version.
antipattern.fix: Update repository signature to require `expected_version`. Pass the version captured at `load()` time. Tests cover the clash path.

antipattern.symptom: Aggregate has `apply_OrderPlaced` but `_apply_ItemAdded` not implemented. Replay raises `AttributeError`. Production stream containing both events cannot be loaded.

root-cause.source: Developer added a new event without its apply handler

antipattern.root-cause: No safety check between event class and apply method.
antipattern.fix: Add a unit test enumerating all event classes and asserting `hasattr(Aggregate, f"_apply_{name}")`. Catch the gap in CI.

antipattern.symptom: Aggregate exposes `self._pending` directly; caller iterates + dispatches before `save()` runs. Persistence failure leaves consumers having processed events that never durably happened.
antipattern.root-cause: No formal boundary between pending events and dispatched events.
antipattern.fix: Hide `_pending`. Provide `collect_pending_events()` that returns + clears. Repository calls it after `save()` succeeds; on failure, events remain on the aggregate for retry.
